---
title: ""
lightbox: true
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

```{=html}
<section class="site-hero" aria-labelledby="hero-title">
  <div class="hero-content">
    <p class="eyebrow">Open trait data for Australian plants</p>
    <h1 id="hero-title">AusTraits</h1>
    <p class="hero-subtitle">A harmonised, open-source database of Australian plant trait data, built for reliable ecological research, biodiversity infrastructure, and conservation decision-making.</p>
    <div class="hero-actions" aria-label="Primary actions">
      <a class="btn-primary" href="https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3568417"><i class="bi bi-cloud-arrow-down"></i> Download data</a>
      <a class="btn-secondary" href="https://traitecoevo.github.io/austraits/"><i class="bi bi-code-slash"></i> Use the R package</a>
      <a class="btn-secondary" href="contributors.html"><i class="bi bi-database-add"></i> Contribute data</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<div class="trust-strip" aria-label="AusTraits key facts">
  <div class="trust-item">
    <span class="trust-value">Nearly 500</span>
    <span class="trust-label">plant traits</span>
  </div>
  <div class="trust-item">
    <span class="trust-value">30,000+</span>
    <span class="trust-label">Australian taxa</span>
  </div>
  <div class="trust-item">
    <span class="trust-value">CC-BY 4.0</span>
    <span class="trust-label">open data licence</span>
  </div>
  <div class="trust-item">
    <span class="trust-value">FAIR</span>
    <span class="trust-label">metadata-rich resource</span>
  </div>
  <div class="trust-item">
    <span class="trust-value">2016+</span>
    <span class="trust-label">community effort</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

AusTraits synthesises plant trait data from field campaigns, published literature, taxonomic monographs, and individual taxon descriptions. The database integrates contributions from functional plant biology, plant physiology, plant taxonomy, conservation biology, and related disciplines.

All AusTraits entries are linked to detailed metadata, harmonised against trait definitions, and checked for consistency. The result is a trusted resource that researchers can use with minimal additional filtering or manipulation. The AusTraits data paper was published in *Scientific Data*: [10.1038/s41597-021-01006-6](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-021-01006-6).

## Access

```{=html}
<p class="section-intro">Start with the access route that matches how you want to use the resource: download the full release, work in R, follow a tutorial, or connect through linked biodiversity platforms.</p>

<div class="card-grid">
  <a class="resource-card" href="https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3568417">
    <span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-cloud-arrow-down"></i></span>
    <h3>Zenodo dataset</h3>
    <p>Download compiled AusTraits releases under an open CC-BY 4.0 licence, grouped under the project concept DOI.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="resource-card" href="https://traitecoevo.github.io/austraits/">
    <span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-braces"></i></span>
    <h3>R interface</h3>
    <p>Use the austraits R package to download, join, filter, explore, and visualise AusTraits data in analysis workflows.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="resource-card" href="https://traitecoevo.github.io/traits.build-book/AusTraits_tutorial.html">
    <span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i></span>
    <h3>Tutorial</h3>
    <p>Follow worked examples for exploring and analysing AusTraits data using the project tooling.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="resource-card" href="https://bie.ala.org.au/species/https://id.biodiversity.org.au/node/apni/2912814#ausTraits">
    <span class="card-icon"><i class="bi bi-diagram-3"></i></span>
    <h3>API and ALA integration</h3>
    <p>Access taxon-level AusTraits summaries through Atlas of Living Australia trait tabs and linked raw downloads.</p>
  </a>
</div>
```

## Structure

```{=html}
<p class="section-intro">AusTraits is a relational, metadata-rich resource. Trait values, taxonomic concepts, methods, sources, and contextual information are kept explicit so users can trace and evaluate the data they use.</p>
```

::: {.image-panel}
![](images/AusTraits_relational_tables.svg){fig-alt="Diagram showing the main relational tables in the AusTraits database."}
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Learn more
Detailed database structure, trait definitions, and metadata fields are documented in the [traits.build book](https://traitecoevo.github.io/traits.build-book/database_structure.html). Trait concepts are published in the [AusTraits Plant Dictionary](https://w3id.org/APD).
:::

## Software

```{=html}
<p class="section-intro">AusTraits is supported by a small ecosystem of open tools and reusable outputs that help researchers build, access, align, and interpret harmonised trait databases.</p>

<div class="software-grid">
  <article class="software-card">
    <img src="images/traits.build_hex.png" alt="traits.build package logo">
    <div>
      <h3>traits.build</h3>
      <p>A data model, workflow, and R package for building harmonised ecological trait databases, including the workflow behind AusTraits.</p>
      <div class="card-actions">
        <a href="https://github.com/traitecoevo/traits.build">GitHub</a>
        <a href="https://traitecoevo.github.io/traits.build-book/">Book</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </article>
  <article class="software-card">
    <img src="images/austraits_hex.png" alt="austraits R package logo">
    <div>
      <h3>austraits</h3>
      <p>An R interface for downloading, joining, filtering, combining, and visualising data from AusTraits and related traits.build databases.</p>
      <div class="card-actions">
        <a href="https://github.com/traitecoevo/austraits">GitHub</a>
        <a href="https://traitecoevo.github.io/austraits/">Documentation</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </article>
  <article class="software-card">
    <img src="images/APCalign_hex.png" alt="APCalign package logo">
    <div>
      <h3>APCalign</h3>
      <p>An R package and Shiny app for aligning Australian vascular plant names to the Australian Plant Census and Australian Plant Names Index.</p>
      <div class="card-actions">
        <a href="https://github.com/traitecoevo/APCalign">GitHub</a>
        <a href="https://doi.org/10.1071/BT24014">Paper</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </article>
  <article class="software-card">
    <img src="images/APD_logo.png" alt="AusTraits Plant Dictionary logo">
    <div>
      <h3>AusTraits Plant Dictionary</h3>
      <p>A formal vocabulary for AusTraits trait concepts, including definitions, allowed values, units, keywords, and links to related ontologies.</p>
      <div class="card-actions">
        <a href="https://w3id.org/APD">Dictionary</a>
        <a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-024-03368-z">Paper</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </article>
</div>
```

### Additional Data Products

Taxonomic descriptions for most Australian plant species are available through online floras. AusTraits includes extracted trait values from these descriptions, including hundreds of thousands of values across key traits. The methods are described in [Coleman et al. 2023](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ecoinf.2023.102312).

AusTraits has also gap-filled near-complete trait tables for core traits such as [life history](https://w3id.org/APD/traits/trait_0030012), [plant growth form](https://w3id.org/APD/traits/trait_0030010), and [woodiness](https://w3id.org/APD/traits/trait_0030019). Protocols and coverage are described in [10.1071/BT23111](https://doi.org/10.1071/BT23111).

## Ongoing Work

```{=html}
<div class="work-grid">
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Data contributions</h3>
    <p>AusTraits continues to integrate newly collected and legacy datasets from researchers, archives, reference books, and taxonomic monographs.</p>
    <a href="contributors.html">Contribute data</a>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Trait coverage</h3>
    <p>The team expands coverage of key traits for disturbance response, climate vulnerability, reproduction, conservation, and environmental assessment.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Synthetic summaries</h3>
    <p>Work is underway to release species and species-by-location trait summaries as reliable building blocks for applied research and decision-making.</p>
  </article>
  <article class="plain-card">
    <h3>Reusable infrastructure</h3>
    <p>The AusTraits workflow supports other groups using traits.build to create harmonised trait databases for new taxa and trait domains.</p>
  </article>
</div>
```

AusTraits has received investment from the Australian Research Data Commons through national research data infrastructure programs, including support from the National Collaborative Research Infrastructure Strategy.
